resources.description: A public resource that can point families toward local aging services and state or community programs.

resources.description: A Medicare comparison tool for certain care providers and settings. Families should confirm what type of setting is being compared.

resources.name: LongTermCare.gov
resources.description: A federal education resource from the Administration for Community Living about long-term care planning and support options.

resources.name: ARCH National Respite Network
resources.description: A respite locator and education resource for family caregivers looking for short-term relief and backup support.

A national referral network that may help families find local community resources.

resources.name: Legal Services Corporation
resources.description: A national starting point for finding civil legal aid organizations. CareInMyCity does not provide legal advice.

resources.name: BenefitsCheckUp
resources.description: A benefits screening tool from the National Council on Aging that can help older adults explore programs they may want to review.

resources.name: Social Security Disability Benefits
resources.description: Official Social Security information about disability benefits. Families should speak with qualified professionals for case-specific guidance.

owned.name: Consumer Support Help
owned.description: Consumer Support Help can help families organize a final expense support request and connect with an appropriate next step. This is not a government resource, insurance carrier, or professional advice service.
owned.disclaimer: Availability and routing may vary. CareInMyCity and Consumer Support Help do not provide medical, legal, financial, or insurance advice.

resources.name: FTC Funeral Rule
resources.description: A Federal Trade Commission consumer resource explaining rights and pricing information related to funeral arrangements.

resources.name: Funeral Consumers Alliance
resources.description: A consumer education resource about funeral planning, costs, and questions families may want to ask.

resources.description: A benefits screening tool that can help older adults and families explore possible programs and supports.

owned.name: Consumer Support Help
